descriptionParagraphs: Who says you can't teach an old dog new tricks?
Passive: Add a new Perk Group to this character's Perk Tree based on what is currently equipped in their hands, which is checked in the following order...
• Mainhand items: always checked first, giving priority to 'Specialist' Classes before Weapon Perk Groups.
• Hybrid Weapons: the character will be given the Perk Group they do not have, or be given one randomly from the applicable Weapon Types if they have neither.
• Offhand items: same as above.
• Unarmed: If no items are equipped, they will gain the Unarmed Perk Group if they do not have it.

•Armor: If armor weight is between 0 and 15 they will receive Light armor perk group. If weight is between 16 and 35 they will receive Medium armor perk group. If weight is over 35 they will receive Heavy armor perk group.
• If the character has all the Perk Groups of their equipped items, they will be given a random Trait Perk Group they do not have.
• When this character reaches level 15, the perk point spent is refunded.
Seize the Initiative!
Active: • Unlocks the Adrenaline skill which puts you first in the turn order for the next round, allowing you another turn before your enemies.
• Costs 1 AP and 20 Fatigue.
Active: • A concoction of secret materials that allows the user to restore up to 25 armor to a target.
Any camp worth its salt will have an alcohol still fashioned out of whatever can be pulled together.
Passive: • Unlocks a chance to get alcohol as a reward when this character is assigned to the Camp Kitchen in camp.
• The rewards increase as you level up. Includes beer, wine and mead.
• Allows alcohol crafting in the Crafting Tent.
Pay close attention at all times, surveying the surroundings and assessing every clue for an insight.
Passive: • Gain +20% Initiative.
Place the bones where they belong before you raise them so that it's less liable to fall apart. Sturdier in both form and function.
Active: • Requires a minor flesh sacrifice to perform the ritual.
• Costs 4 AP and 15 Fatigue.
You're as capable with one hand as the other!
Passive: • When your offhand is free, follow up attacks with Hand to Hand. Gain +5 Melee Skill and +10 Melee Defense if both hands are free.
• If using a Buckler, Mummy Shield or Parrying Dagger, follow up attacks with Buckler Bash or Stab instead.
• When dual wielding other weapons, the weight penalty is reduced by 33%.
Expertise in making ammunition lets you strip it down to pack the parts for storage, it also lets you draw out arrows faster.
Passive: • Increases maximum ammunition storage by 10 and Ranged skill by +5%.
Using blankets and ropes to group large bundles of ammunition together you are able to carry far more. These bundles can also be used to shield yourself from enemy fire.
Passive: • Increases maximum ammunition storage by 20 and Ranged Defense by +5%.
Through extensive study of the human form, you learn how to fashion replacement parts from wood and metal.
Passive: • Unlocks the creation of prosthetic body parts in the crafting tent to help your mercenaries resolve their permanent Injuries.
Hold your ground!
Passive: • Gain a stacking +5 Melee Skill, Ranged Skill and Melee Defense as long as you have not moved during your turn. You will start combat with 1 stack.
• If you were to receive Stagger instead remove a stack.
By watching your opponent's aim, you have more time to react.
Passive: • When being attacked with ranged weapons, gain 1 + 10% of your base Ranged Defense as additional Ranged Defense per tile that the attacker is away.
• Always gain at least +10 to Ranged Defense when attacked from range.
• If you're armed with a ranged weapon and a ranged attack misses you, gain a chance to fire back at your attacker. The chance is based on Ranged Defense.
Raise a ramshackle thrall from some bones only barely ordered. Though lacking in strength or bodily integrity, it shall obey.
Active: • Requires a small flesh sacrifice to perform the ritual.
• Costs 3 AP and 10 Fatigue.
Striking from the shadows allows you to bypass enemy defenses and strike for their undefended weak points.
Passive: • Grants +20% maximum and minimum damage while Hidden by being within a bush or smoke.
• Assassins who specialize in the art of stealth gain +30% instead.
• Master assassins more deadly in stealth and killing gain +50%.
Your confidence affects your performance.
Passive: • Gain +10% Melee Skill, Ranged Skill, Melee Defense, and Ranged Defense when at Confident morale.
• These bonuses reduce as your morale state decreases: +5% at Steady, -5% when Breaking and -10% when Fleeing. Removes the negative effects of the 'Cocky' trait.
You're a well-rounded athlete!
Passive: • Reduce the Fatigue cost of all your skills by a percentage equal to 20% of your maximum usable Fatigue after gear weight is accounted for.
Master combat with axes and destroying shields.
Passive: • Skills build up 25% less Fatigue.
• Split Shield damage is increased by 50% when used with axes.
• Round Swing gains +5% chance to hit.
• The Longaxe no longer has a penalty for attacking targets directly adjacent.
Years of practice lead this character to fall back to their instincts and basic training under extreme stress.
Passive: • When this character takes damage, they will gain a stacking +5 Melee Defense until the start of their next turn.
• If they take damage while Fleeing, they have a chance to rally. The chance is equal to half their Resolve.
• Also grants +10% training speed in camp.
Extensive training in circus maneuvers allows you to move out of harm's way with astonishing speed.
Passive: • If you have taken the Footwork or Leap perk, they gain 1 range.
Honor doesn't win you fights, stabbing the enemy where it hurts does.
Passive: • Increase damage to Hitpoints by 5% and armor penetration by 3% for each ally surrounding and distracting your target. Works with any weapon and skill.
Use your built up inertia to wreak havoc on the second swing.

Passive: • After performing Round Swing, Swing, Thresh, Reap, Castigate, Shatter, Harvest and Double Swing you can perform a followup for -50% Fatigue and Action Points. Swing Damage is reduced by -25% and for the rest of the skills by -50%.
• Moving, ending your turn or waiting will all remove this effect.
Extra pockets.
Passive: •Unlocks two extra bag slots to carry all your favorite things.
• Non-two-handed items placed in bags no longer give a penalty to Maximum Fatigue.
Balancing your armor can allow protect against different threats.
Passive: • Gain +15 Ranged Defense. For every 5 points of armor and helmet weight decrease this bonus by 1, but gain 1 Melee Defense up to a maximum of 15.
Through familiarity and training with your weapon, you know just how your projectiles will fly.

Passive: • Crossbows gain a scaling bonus to armor penetration depending on the distance to the target with the highest bonus in melee and the lowest at maximum range for the relevant skill. Shoot Bolt gains up to 20%, while Shoot Heavy Bolt and Shoot Stake gain up to +30% instead.
• The range penalty to hitchance is reduced by -2 per tile for all ranged weapons.
• Additionally, Handgonnes gain +1 range.
Master the art of bandaging mid battle using any cloth available.
Passive: • Unlocks the Bandage skill which allows you to bandage allies without a bandage.
• Can bandage while in a zone of control.
• Costs 6 AP and 5 Fatigue.
Lets hope your enemies like fighting in the shade.
Passive: •Sling Stone will now Debilitate on headshots and Baffle on body shots.
• Fire Handgonne will apply Shellshocked on headshots.
• Sling Heavy Stone will stun on headshots.
• Shoot Bolt and Shoot Stake will automatically reload on headshots.
Glide through battle like a leaf in a stream.
Passive: • Once per turn, upon killing an opponent, reduce current Fatigue by -10% of characters base maximum Fatigue (before penalties due to armor weight are applied).
Specialize in heavy armor!
Passive: • Armor damage taken is reduced by a percentage equal to 5% of the current total armor value of both body and head armor.
• The heavier your armor and helmet the more you benefit. Damage taken cannot be reduced below 20% of the total value.
• Does not affect damage from mental attacks or status effects.
'Being aware of your surroundings can make or break a fight.'
Passive: • Has a 50% lower chance to inflict friendly fire. Stacks with the 'Team Player'.
• Knock Back will now apply Taunt effect.
• Knock Back, Repel and Hook skills have 100% chance to hit when targeting an allied character and will not apply negative effects or damage when used on an ally.
Active: • Unlocks the Taunt skill which forces a targeted opponent take offensive actions instead of defensive ones.

• Will also apply the Taunt effect which will make all enemies more likely to target you until the start of your next turn. Enemies will be more likely to target you by 15% for every 10 points of resolve with a maximum bonus of 150%.
• Costs 4 AP and 15 Fatigue.
Wade into the thick of battle, dodging blades from behind and above, aware of all around you at the heart of the battle.
Passive: • Being surrounded no longer affects this character's defences at all.
• Upgraded version of Underdog, immune to Strength In Numbers.
Active: • Transform into a powerful bear, doubles your Hitpoints. Requires free hands.
Endless communion with the animals has won you the trust of the bears.
Active: • Adds a pet bear to your stash, make sure you have space before taking this perk.
• Unlocks an event to tame bears in the woods.
Become a berserker
Passive:This character background changes into a berserker, losing all current perks.
RAAARGH!
Passive: • Once per turn, upon killing an enemy 4 Action Points are immediately restored.
• Characters cannot regain more than their maximum Action Points.
Gain rage stacks over the course of a battle.
Passive: • Each stack increases Damage by 1%, Initiative by 1, Resolve by 1 and reduces damage received by 2%.
• Gain 1 stack each time you take damage, and 3 stacks each time you kill.
• Lose 2 rage stacks per turn.
• Can only accumulate a maximum of 40 stacks and 70% damage reduction.
The bigger they are...

Passive: • When fighting beasts, gain an additional modifier that deals bonus damage based on the percentage of the target's missing Hitpoints. The more Hitpoints the target has lost, the more bonus damage is dealt, up to the total damage of the attack.
• When not fighting beasts, gain +10% ranged weapon damage but no additional modifier.
• Only works with ranged weapons.
Passive: • Getting a horse accustomed to the feel of a bit and a bridle, makes it easier to control the movements. This increases defenses in battle by 25% of the rider's Melee Defense.
Diligent practice with the hammer each day has proven to be equally good at crafting armor and finding it's weak points.
Passive: • While using a Blacksmith's Hammer gain +12 chance to hit and +30% effectiveness vs armor.
• When taking Hammer Mastery you will also gain 10% bonus damage.

• For other weapons classified as One Handed Hammers, you can get a scaling percentage of these bonuses, starting from 25% and increasing gradually to 100% by 5% for each level with the maximum bonus gained at level 15.
Hold yourself in a way that makes you seem more trouble than its worth, lean into trees, place objects between you and an enemy, and hide behind allies.
Passive: • Makes enemies 50% less likely to attack you instead of an ally.
• Grants +5 Ranged Defense and +3 Melee Defense.
Take joy in the suffering of others.

Passive: • Gain Melee Skill, Ranged Skill, Fatigue Recovery and Resolve for each character that is Bleeding or has a temporary Injury on the field. Double the bonus if you're adjacent to bleeding enemies.
• The fatigue recovery bonus cannot exceed 5.
There's wolves, bears, nachzehrers, and you. All beings of vicious slaughter.
Passive: • 50% of the skill's fatigue cost is translated to Damage and Chance to hit. This accounts for any fatigue reduction or increase.
Even if your strikes don't penetrate the opponent's armor, you'll make sure they feel it.

Passive: • Increases hitpoint damage and always inflict a minimum amount of damage to hitpoints equal to 10% of the armor damage done to the target. Attacks that don't deal armor damage or fully penetrate the target's armor will not receive any bonuses, and the bonus damage is subject to damage reductions from the target.
Catch your enemies by suprise
Passive: • When in a hiding place, such as high grass, gain a bonus of +10 to Melee Skill, Ranged Skill, and Melee and Ranged Defense.
Learn to keep your shield up.
Passive: • The first two times you end a turn without Shieldwall activated, gain Shieldwall.
• Gain 10% damage reduction while using a shield.
Master the art of archery and pelting your opponents with arrows from afar.
Passive: • Skills build up 25% less Fatigue.
• View range and maximum firing range with bows is increased by +1.
All this exercise has you in great shape.
Passive: • Fatigue is increased by 25% of your Base Fatigue. This bonus will also offset Initiative penalties from wearing armor.
• Weapons with extra Fatigue build-up on skills have this penalty reduced by 1
You've never met a problem that money couldn't solve.
Active: • Throw coins at your enemies to bribe them away from battle, only works on humans. The cost is the XP that would be granted by killing the enemy, to the power of 1.3.
• Costs variable AP and variable Fatigue based on the XP.
Use your knowledge of undeath to bring your mercenaries back from the dead.
Passive: • Gives 66% chance for ally to come back as undead
Mastering the art of leaning against a wall with a big stick has it's benefits.
Specialist Weapon Perk: • Gain +12 chance to hit and +5 fatigue per hit while using a Wooden Stick or Bludgeon.
• When taking Mace Mastery gain 10% damage.

• For other weapons classified as One-Handed Mace, you can get a scaling percentage of these bonuses, starting from 25% and increasing gradually to 100% by 5% for each level with the maximum bonus gained at level 15.
Your muscles are an unstoppable force, shattering armor with every strike!
Passive: • Effectiveness vs. Armor is increased by +10% for any weapon used.
Nailed it!
Passive: • The penalty to hitchance when shooting at a target you have no clear line of fire to is reduced from 75% to 25% for any ranged weapons and Aimed Shot will not have a penalty at all.
• This Character's Vision is increased by +1.
Active: • Unleash a cone of blistering heat from your palms, can hit up to 9 targets for 10-15 damage.
Your skill on the butcher's block seems to be easily translated to chopping limbs on the battlefield.
Passive: • Gain +12 chance to hit and +2 Bleed damage while using a Butcher's Cleaver.
• When taking Cleaver Mastery you will also gain 10% bonus damage.

• For other weapons classified as Cleavers, but not Whips, you can get a scaling percentage of these bonuses, starting from 25% and increasing gradually to 100% by 5% for each level with the maximum bonus gained at level 15.
Active: • Call down lightning upon your enemies. Grants a 10% chance to unleash lightning on each unit within 4 tiles. Must have empty hands and it must be raining.
Master the art of concealment.
Active: • Unlocks Camoflauge skill which allows you to blend in with your surroundings and remain Hidden from the enemy for three turns, or until your next attack.
• Costs 8 AP and 40 Fatigue.
There is a fine art to cooking, skills and ingredients that are not obvious but unlock a world of flavor or preserve food for longer.
Passive: • Unlocks food crafting in the crafting tent.
• Allows the creation of porridge, puddings, pies, meat curing, fruit drying and ration making.
If it bleeds, we can kill it.
Passive: • Gain 20% damage when attacking a Bleeding enemy.
Active: • Unlocks the Prepare to Bleed skill which primes your next strike to inflict 5 Bleed damage for the next two turns.
• Costs 2 AP and 15 Fatigue.
• When Dual Wielding cleavers or whips, both attacks apply Bleed.
Active: • Unlocks the Prepare to Graze skill which primes your next strike to inflict 2 Bleed damage for the next five turns.
• Costs 2 AP and 15 Fatigue.
Unleash an arcing barrage of lightning!
Active: • Unlocks Chain Lightning skill which inflicts damage to up to 3 enemies, dealing 15-40 damage which ignores armor.
• Requires a staff.
• Costs 8 AP and 50 Fatigue.
Active: • Fire a bolt of lightning at an enemy that can arc to two adjacent enemies.
Learn to better focus your art so it is less draining on your body.
Passive: • All skills that raise dead build up 30% less Fatigue.
Obtain mastery over chants.
Passive: • Chants build up 25% less Fatigue.
• The effects of the Heightened Senses, Disharmony and Fury chants are increased by 10%.
Use your way with song to cheer on an ally.
Active: • Unlocks the Cheer On skill which Grants +2 Action Points to an ally adjacent to you.
• Can only be used with an Instrument or Banner equipped.
• Costs 4 AP and 30 Fatigue.
Active: • A magic circle that protects all allies within casting. Leaving the circle breaks the protection.
Active: • A powerful cocktail of ingredients provides a trance like state, granting the banner effect, and drums of war effects to everyone in the company at the end of the user's turn.
A well honed mind can perceive more in each instant, making time appear slower in key instants of need.
Passive: • An additional +10% of any damage ignores armor.
• Does not work if you are Dazed, Drunk, Hungover, Taunted, Staggered, Horrified, Concussed or Wavering.
• Doubles the armor ignore bonus if under the effect of the following potions: Iron Will, Lionheart, Cat, Second Wind or Hexen Ichor.
Master cleavers to inflict gruesome wounds.
Passive: • Skills build up 25% less Fatigue.
• Bleeding damage inflicted by cleavers and whips is doubled to 10 and 20 per turn, respectively.
• Disarm only has half the penalty to hit.
Learn to scale cliffs with unmatched speed!
Active: • Unlocks the Climb skill which allows you to climb sheer cliffs up to five levels of height in one action.
• Does not trigger attacks of opportunity.
• Costs 3 AP and 20 Fatigue.
Passive: • By training the horse's posture, advanced movements become easier to perform and less draining. Increases Fatigue by 25%
Bring it on!
Passive: • Hitpoints are increased by 25%, which reduces the chance to sustain debilitating Injuries on hit.
Train to keep your cool, under even the most challenging circumstances.
Passive: • Makes you immune to Stuns
Conduct profane rituals meant to stave off the passage of time, further preserving the means of your craft.
Passive: • Reduces the daily medicine cost of maintaining Human Corpses, Bones, and other Carrion by 50%.
Passive: • Gives character self control while transformed.
This characters skill with words makes their trade proposals more acceptable.
Passive: • Increases Barter skill by +1%, improving all buy and sell prices and rewards from trade contracts.
• They also generate +10 Crowns each day through their investments and an additional +1 Crowns per level.
• Unfortunately, their negotiation skill also increases their daily pay rate by +25%.
'Archers HOLD!'
Active: • Grants the Coordinated Volleys skill which grants+10 ranged attack to all allies within 4 tiles for one turn.
• Costs 4 AP and 20 Fatigue.
Like a beast of prey, you hunt the weak.
Passive: • Inflict an additional 20% damage against targets that are Debilitated or have sustained any injury effects, like a broken arm.

• Also grants +10% damage against targets who suffer from any of the following effects: Baffled, Dazed, Distracted, Grappled, Netted, Rooted, Shellshocked, Sleeping, Staggered, Stunned, Vulnerable, Webbed, or Withered.
Cripple your enemies!
Passive: • Lowers the threshold to inflict Injuries by 33% for both melee and ranged attacks.
• Undead cannot be injured, but you gain +10% damage against them.
Master crossbows and firearms, and learn where to aim best.
Passive: • Skills build up 25% less Fatigue.
• An additional 20% of damage inflicted with crossbows ignores armor.
• Handgonnes now require 6 Action Points to reload and can be fired every turn instead of every other turn.
Master swift and deadly daggers.
Passive: • Skills build up 25% less Fatigue.
• Stab, Puncture, and Deathblow Action Point cost is reduced to 3, allowing for an additional attack each turn.
• Throw Knife chance to distract on head hit is increased by 25%.
Every person has their price.
Active: • Unlocks the Danger Pay skill which allows you to target any ally that is paid a wage within three tiles and Spend 10 times the unit's daily wage to set them at Confident morale.
• Upon receiving extra payment, the recipient becomes Motivated for three turns.
• Motivated grants 10% more damage, 5% more Ranged and Melee Skill, and 15% more Resolve.
• Costs 2 AP and 4 Fatigue.
Disapparate from your current location and reappear on the other side of the battlefield
Active: • Unlocks the Darkflight skill which lets you teleport up to six tiles away.
• Costs 4 AP and 40 Fatigue.
Assault the senses of your target with a flurry of colorful movement and sounds.
Active: • Unlocks the Daze skill which applies the Bewildered effect on hit.
• Chance to hit is based on Ranged Skill and has a range of six tiles.
• Bewildered enemies have their Initiative, Fatigue, and damage reduced by -50%.
• If you have the Taunt perk, chance to hit is doubled.
• Costs 5 AP and 20 Fatigue.
Active: • Assault the senses of your target with a conjured flurry of colorful sparks, whirs, and pops. Such an astonishing display is sure to leave anyone too Bewildered to fight effectively.
Does no damage.
Thread the needle from this distance? No sweat.
Specialist Weapon Perk: • Gain +12 chance to hit and 1 additional range while using a Warbow.
• When taking Bow Mastery gain 10% damage.

• For other weapons classified as Longbows, you can get a scaling percentage of these bonuses, starting from 25% and increasing gradually to 100% by 5% for each level with the maximum bonus gained at level 15.
• The additional range gained is still subject to vision penalties.
Your fingers turn ghostly and pass through steel and flesh, tearing at the soul of your victim.
Active: • Unlocks the Death Touch skill which uses your Melee Skill to hit.
• Damage scales with current Initiative and ignores armor.
• Costs 5 AP and 20 Fatigue.
Ankles, hands, elbows and knees, you've learned how to fight dirty to secure victory.

Active: • Unlocks the Debilitate skill which can prime your next attack to Cripple a target for three turns, reducing their ability to inflict damage by -25% and increasing the damage they take by +15%.
• Costs 3 AP and 10 Fatigue. Works on enemy targets hit.
In Pain we find the truth of ourselves. We have no identity beyond servitude, our glory is agony.
Passive: • While your face is obscured by a cultist hood, gain +15% of your total Resolve as a bonus to melee and Ranged Defense.
• Also works with cultist leather hood, leather helmet, sack, decayed sack helm, warlock hood or mask of davkul.
• Unlocks a crafting recipe to make cultist hoods and sacks.
Learn to better deflect hits to the side instead of blocking them head on.
Passive: • Shield damage received is reduced by -50% to a minimum of 1.
Active: • Hailstone can now ignore 65% of armor and inflicts 125% damage to armor.

Passive: • Using loud noises and sudden movements, the horse is trained to not react to the sounds of battle until the rider tells it to. This increases melee attack in battle by 25% of the rider's melee attack
What could stand before you now?
Passive: • All damage inflicted is increased by 10%.
A very disagreeable and cacophonous chant can makes it almost impossible for enemies to focus and concentrate properly.

Active: • Unlocks the Disharmony chant which prevents enemies from enforcing Zones of Control while adjacent to the Vala and enemies within 3 tiles of the Vala will be afflicted by a malus to Initiative.
• The debuff to Initiative increases by 1% for every 15 points of Resolve and Fatigue the Vala has.
• The reduction weakens by 25% per one tile the target is away from the Vala.
• Costs 3 AP and 30 Fatigue.

Passive: • This character spends a lot of time alone outside camp, staring into the distance and smoking strange bark. Allows the user to have distant visions of towns. Each morning this character is in reserves grants you insight into a specific town's current events and buildings.
Too fast for you!
Passive: • Gain 15% of this character's current Initiative as a bonus to Melee and Ranged Defense.
• Gain a further 15% bonus if your maximum Hitpoints are closer to 50. This additional bonus gradually drops as your maximum Hitpoints increase to 100.
Intimate knowledge of dog care and conditions required for breeding allows you to successfully breed dogs.
Passive: • Unlocks a recipe in the crafting tent for breeding dogs.
You have mastered the art of training dogs.
Active: • In the heat of battle your hounds will respond and react to your commands, order them to attack or defend allies.
• Costs 3 AP and 0 Fatigue.
Your way with animals extends so far as turning those dogs that would do you harm.
Active: • By spending 1 item of food you can make a dog flee from battle.
• The food item is chosen at random and the perk does not work if you have less than 25 food.
• Costs 4 AP and 0 Fatigue.
You have a way with dogs, the bond you form with them imbues each dog with more confidence and strength, knowing that you are there gives them strength.
Passive: • Grants each dog you unleash the perks: Colossus, Fortified Mind, and Underdog.
Master the art of following up for devastating effects.
Passive: • After landing a hit, the next attack this turn does 25% overall damage and it costs 20% less Fatigue than normal.
• If the attack misses, the effect is lost.
• When Dual Wielding daggers, if both weapons successfully hit, the next mainhand attack costs -1 AP and deals +10% damage.
Master the art of fighting with a weapon in each hand.
Passive: • Reduces off-hand weapon damage malus from -66% to -50% when dual wielding.
Become one with your weapon and go for the weak spots!
Passive: • When holding a Buckler or Mummy Shield in your offhand an extra +25% of damage ignores armor on Melee Attacks.

Active: Unlocks the Flourish skill, which will increase fatigue cost by 50% and as long as you are faster than your target grants a chance on each attack to ignore armor completely based on the initiative difference between you and your target. As long as you are faster than the target, any attack that inflicts at least 1 point of damage to Hitpoints triggers a morale check for the opponent with a penalty equal to 20% of the Initiative difference between you and the target.
Arranging gear in just the right way, you're able to squeeze more things into the same space.
Passive: • Grants 8 additional stash spaces and +6 Fatigue.
Pull at the strings of a single creation, compelling them to linger among you as an obedient guardian.
Active: • Enthralled dead act defensively and attempt to protect you from threats.
• Costs 4 AP and 15 Fatigue.
With a trick of the light, captivate your target in such a way that they can't help but approach you.
Active: • Unlocks the Entice skill which pulls your target one tile towards you with a 100% chance to Stagger on hit.
• Chance to hit is based on Ranged Skill and has a range of nine tiles.
• Costs 6 AP and 10 Fatigue.
Taverns have taught you that instruments can be used to make spin tunes, bash heads and block a thrown vegetable.
Passive: • Increases the effects of Song of Life and War Chant by 3 Hitpoints and 2 Fatigue, respectively.
• Gain +10 Melee Defense when using Musical Instruments.
Others see it as magic, but your deft hands know better.
Passive: • Grants a baseline 99% chance to Break Free from rooting effects, such as nets and webs.
• Reduces the Action Point cost of Break Free by 1.
'Excuse me'

Active: • Enables the character to move swiftly and safely through any Zone of Control for one turn. The first attack against you will be an automatic miss. Can only be used if your total armor weight is 30 or less.
• Costs 4 AP and 20 Fatigue.
Long, slow, deep rending scratches that leave many bleeding grazes.
Active: • Unlocks the Prepare to Graze skill which primes your next strike to inflict 2 Bleed damage for the next five turns.
• Costs 2 AP and 15 Fatigue.
Time spent assessing an enemy, finding the arteries, will pay off in blood shed on the field.
Active: • Unlocks the Prepare to Bleed skill which primes your next strike to inflict 5 Bleed damage for the next two turns.
• Costs 2 AP and 15 Fatigue.
• When Dual Wielding cleavers or whips, both attacks apply Bleed.
Yield to the dark power, let it ooze from your pores. With the fell energy now unrestrained, you can further project your art of rousing the dead.
Passive: • Increases range of all skills that raise dead by one tile.
Organize the remains before you raise it so that your creation is better suited to the work it is given. Sturdier in both form and function.
Active: • Requires 20 Hitpoints to summon and 1 human carrion.
• Taking this perk adds +30 medicine to your stash and increases total medicine by +30.
• Costs 4 AP and 20 Fatigue.
Style over substance.
Passive: • Tabards, cloaks and helmet vanity layers weigh nothing. This impacts Nimble and Lithe.
Adapt to your opponent's moves!
Passive: • Gain an additional stacking +10% chance to hit with each attack that misses an opponent.
• Upon landing a hit, the character will also recover 1 Action Point for every stack up to a maximum of 3 and the bonus will be reset. The refund cannot exceed the maximum Action Points.
Enforce your position at the top of the food chain.
Passive: • Grants bonuses to Melee Skill, Ranged Skill and maximum damage while fighting Beasts. This bonus increases with every Beast kill made by this character.

• Increase per kill: - Nachzehrers | +0.16% - Skin Ghoul | +0.5% - Unhold (all types) | +0.5% - Rock Unhold | +2% - Lindwurm | +1% - Serpent | +0.5% - Stollwurm | +2.5% - Direwolf | +0.2% - White Direwolf | +0.5% - Hyena | +0.2% - Enraged Hyena | +0.5% - Webknecht | +0.125% - Spider Eggs | +0.5% - Redback | +1%

• At 15% or more increases the likelihood of receiving extremely difficult legendary contracts to hunt the following legendary Beasts: Redback Spiders, Rock Unholds, Skin Ghouls, Stollwurms and White Direwolfs.
We live in a society.
Passive: • Grants bonuses to Melee Skill, Ranged Skill and maximum damage while fighting law-abiding citizens. This bonus increases with every Lawful kill made by this character.

• Increase per kill: - Footman | +0.125% - Zweihander | +0.25% - Billman | +0.125% - Arbalester | +0.125% - Standard Bearer | +0.5% - Sergeant | +0.5% - Fencer | +0.25% - Halberdier | +0.25% - Slinger | +0.125% - Knight | +1% - Elite Footman | +0.125% - Pollaxe | +0.125% - Sureshot | +0.125%
• Increases the likelihood of encountering Lawful champions.
• 5% chance to get any items from slain enemies, that otherwise might not be available to loot. Stacks with itself and effects of a similar nature.
Big or small, greenskins lack the strength of will.
Passive: • Grants bonuses to Melee Skill, Ranged Skill, maximum damage while fighting Greenskins. This bonus increases with every Greenskin kill made by this character.
